failure_modes.text: A flawed problem definition can drive elegant work toward irrelevant or costly solutions.
failure_modes.source_quote: a flawed problem definition is a primary pitfall leading to irrelevant or costly solutions
failure_modes.mitigation: Before investing in analysis, verify the problem statement: ask three stakeholders to independently state the problem and reconcile the differences before proceeding.

failure_modes.text: Teams can jump into analysis too early and boil the ocean before the real structure of the problem is clear.
failure_modes.source_quote: A human inclination to rush into data gathering and complex analysis without achieving clarity on the underlying problem structure.
failure_modes.mitigation: Timebox the framing phase: set a clear boundary between problem definition and analysis, and do not allow analysis to start until the frame is tested and agreed.

failure_modes.text: One favored frame can become a hammer that tortures reality to fit the model instead of exposing better cuts.
failure_modes.source_quote: To the man with only a hammer, everything looks like a nail
failure_modes.mitigation: Rotate frames deliberately: after settling on a primary frame, force a pass through at least one structurally different frame to expose what the first one hides.

premortem_questions.text: What other disciplines or frameworks might view this problem entirely differently?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What other disciplines or frameworks might view this problem entirely differently?

premortem_questions.text: What evidence would conclusively prove our current framing wrong?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What evidence would conclusively prove our current framing/hypothesis wrong?

premortem_questions.text: What is the single core question we are trying to answer?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What is the single core question we are trying to answer?

heuristics.text: Test several cuts through the problem before committing to the current unit of analysis or point of view.
heuristics.source_quote: Different frames often yield different insights, necessitating the testing of several approaches.

heuristics.text: Use a structured problem statement so owner, constraints, actors, and success criteria are explicit before analysis deepens.
heuristics.source_quote: A structured framework for rigorous problem definition, ensuring clarity on the Trouble, Owner, Success criteria, Constraints, and Actors involved.

heuristics.text: Keep a one-day-answer mentality so reframing sharpens the next decision instead of becoming endless conceptual drift.

curation_notes.summary: The source is naturally chunk-ready on anti-single-frame discipline, precise problem definition, and reframing questions that sharpen decisions.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor creativity-process sprawl because the raw source is stronger on boundary choice and frame-testing pressure.
open_questions: The third heuristic uses the one-day-answer prompt to preserve action pressure even though the source discusses it across several neighboring problem-solving passages.

allies: The raw allies section is strong on systems thinking and metacognitive frame-checking for later relation preservation.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section is strongest on confirmation-bias lock and familiar-frame substitution pressures.
structured_tensions: The source includes an explicit structured tension block around confirmation-bias and representativeness-heuristic, but only the current-vocabulary-safe portion should survive into Wave 3.
